medical_history.history_of_present_illness: diagnosed for a bilateral sensorineural deafness BSD at 12 months old, during his childhood he developed a muscular weakness, polydipsia, nocturnal enuresis, hyperuricemia with a metabolic alkalosis and high levels of renin

other_tests.findings: Profound bilateral sensorineural hearing loss with absence of evoked response to stimulation up to 90 dB
Identified two novel missense mutations p.Arg8Gly (c.22C>G) and p.Thr36Asn (c.107C>A) in exon 1 of BSND gene
Confirmed the presence of mutations p.Arg8Gly (c.22C>G) and p.Thr36Asn (c.107C>A) in BSND gene

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the identification of two novel homozygous missense mutations (p.Arg8Gly and p.Thr36Asn) in the BSND gene, which encodes barttin, a subunit of ClC-Kb and ClC-Ka channels. These mutations were found in patients presenting with hypokalemia, hyperuricaemia, metabolic alkalosis, high levels of plasmatic renin and aldosterone, and sensorineural deafness, which are characteristic clinical symptoms of Bartter syndrome type IV. The pathogenic effect of these mutations was supported by bioinformatics tools (PolyPhen-2 and SIFT) and the conservation of the affected residues across species.
